name: Roman site of Los Banales y Biota — circular from Sádaba Castle
name_original: Yacimiento romano de Los Bañales y Biota — circular desde el Castillo de Sádaba
region_intro: Touring cycling around Sádaba offers routes through the plains of Cinco Villas and near the unique Bardenas Reales Natural Park. This region features a semi-desert landscape with distinctive rock formations, gorges, and plateaus. The terrain provides varied cycling experiences, from flatter sections to areas with more undulating profiles. Sádaba is situated in a landscape that combines agricultural plains with the dramatic natural features of the nearby park. Best touring cycling routes around Sádaba The most popular touring cycling route is Roman site of Los Banales y Biota — circular from Sádaba Castle, a 12.6 miles (20.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 38 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores historical sites and the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Deer Pass – Castildetierra Hill loop from El Paso, a moderate 25.4 miles (40.9 km) path. This route traverses the distinctive semi-desert landscape of Bardenas Reales, featuring unique geological formations. Local touring cyclists also love the Monument to the Bardenas Shepherd – View of the Bardenas Reales loop from El Paso, a 18.0 miles (29.0 km) trail leading through the dramatic scenery of Bardenas Reales, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Sádaba is defined by semi-desert landscapes, unique rock formations, and agricultural plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy to difficult. The routes in Sádaba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 90 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Sádaba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Sádaba?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes available around Sádaba. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options ranging from easy to difficult.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for touring cycling in Sádaba?
A: The terrain around Sádaba offers a diverse experience. You'll find routes through the agricultural plains of Cinco Villas, as well as more dramatic semi-desert landscapes near the Bardenas Reales Natural Park. This includes distinctive rock formations, gorges, and plateaus, providing varied cycling profiles from flatter sections to undulating areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Sádaba?
A: Sádaba's climate, particularly its proximity to the semi-desert Bardenas Reales, suggests that spring and autumn would be ideal for touring cycling. These seasons generally offer milder temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Sádaba area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Sádaba are designed as circular loops. For example, you can explore the historical sites on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994009242/roman-site-of-los-banales-y-biota-circular-from-sadaba-castle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman site of Los Banales y Biota — circular from Sádaba Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or experience the unique landscapes of Bardenas Reales on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38285579" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Deer Pass – Castildetierra Hill loop from El Paso&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes around Sádaba?
A: The touring cycling routes around Sádaba offer a range of difficulty levels. Out of 20 available routes, 5 are considered easy, 12 are moderate, and 3 are difficult, ensuring options for cyclists of all experience levels.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while cycling near Sádaba?
A: Cycling near Sádaba offers views of unique natural features, especially within the Bardenas Reales Natural Park. You can encounter dramatic semi-desert landscapes, distinctive rock formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/945742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castildetierra&lt;/a&gt;, and various gorges and plateaus. The area also features viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3927882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardena Blanca Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1170992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardenas Reales Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; itself.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks along the touring cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Sádaba and its surroundings are rich in history. You can cycle past significant Roman-era monuments like the Mausoleum of Los Atilios, and the town itself boasts the magnificent 13th-century Sádaba Castle and the elegant Church of Santa María. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994009242/roman-site-of-los-banales-y-biota-circular-from-sadaba-castle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman site of Los Banales y Biota — circular from Sádaba Castle&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores historical sites.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints on touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes, particularly those venturing into the Bardenas Reales Natural Park, offer stunning viewpoints. Notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3927882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardena Blanca Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1170994" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de las Cortinillas&lt;/a&gt;, which provide panoramic vistas of the unique semi-desert landscape.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Sádaba?
A: While specific parking areas for cyclists are not detailed, Sádaba, as a municipality, generally offers public parking options. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient and secure places to park your vehicle when starting a cycling tour.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes using public transport in Sádaba?
A: Information regarding public transport specifically accommodating bicycles in Sádaba is limited. For routes starting directly from Sádaba, it's generally more practical to arrive by car or cycle directly to the starting point. If planning to use public transport, it's recommended to verify bicycle carriage policies with local bus or train operators in advance.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for cycling in the Sádaba area, especially in Bardenas Reales?
A: While general touring cycling on public roads and trails around Sádaba typically doesn't require permits, special regulations might apply within protected areas like the Bardenas Reales Natural Park. It's always best to check the official website of the Bardenas Reales Natural Park for the latest information on access rules and any required permits for cycling within its boundaries.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes around Sádaba?
A: Yes, Sádaba offers easy touring cycling routes suitable for families. These routes often feature flatter sections through agricultural plains, providing a more relaxed experience. Look for routes designated as 'easy' in the komoot guide for suitable options.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Sádaba?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unique semi-desert landscapes of Bardenas Reales, the varied terrain, and the opportunity to combine outdoor activity with historical exploration.

Q: Are dogs allowed on touring cycling routes near Sádaba?
A: Regulations regarding dogs on cycling routes can vary, especially in natural parks. While many general trails may permit dogs on a leash, it's crucial to verify specific rules for protected areas like the Bardenas Reales Natural Park. Always ensure your dog is under control and that you clean up after them.
tips: At the top of a small hill in the town of Sádaba stands the beautiful Castle of the same name in late-medieval style with Cistercian decorations. Without a doubt, we must visit it if we are in the area. It is also worth taking a walk around the town, since it hides wonderful corners throughout the old town, as well as the church of Santa María.
We visited the Roman site of Los Bañales, a magnificent site with a forum, baths, living quarters and a spectacular water supply aqueduct.
From this point we will have magnificent views of the aqueduct belonging to the Roman City of Los Bañales

The beautiful town of Biota is located in the Comarca de las Cinco Villas, on the banks of the Arba de Luesia River, where it is worth noting the parish church of San Miguel Arcángel, a magnificent Romanesque-style construction, the Palace of the Viscount of Biota or the "Little" of Ice.

waypoints.name: Church of San Miguel Arcángel of Biota
